steps.title: Identify the explanatory signal
steps.content: The word particularly introduces the underlined phrase right after the statement that advertisers hesitated to jump into a new space. This word marks the phrase as providing a specific reason for the hesitation mentioned just before.

steps.title: Match the function to the choices
steps.content: The underlined phrase identifies a concrete obstacle—TV manufacturing stalled due to WWII—that explains why advertisers were reluctant to invest. A focuses on the wrong subject (programming popularity), B fabricates a comparison between radio and TV origins not in the text, and C describes tactics employed later (how broadcasters tried to persuade sponsors), not the initial hesitation. This matches choice D — D is correct.
